description: Fix misleading isReady() build warning message in opentabs-plugin build

userStories.title: Improve isReady() build warning to not imply the method is missing
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the build warning about isReady() returning false to accurately describe the situation so that I understand I need to add an authentication check rather than thinking my code is broken.
acceptanceCriteria: The warning message no longer says 'until isReady() is implemented' — because the method IS implemented, it just returns false
The new message clearly states that isReady() returned false and the plugin will report as unavailable in the browser
The message hints that the developer should add a real authentication check (e.g., check for session cookies, DOM indicators, or page globals)
userStories.notes:
The fix is a single string change in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts around line 1060. The current message is:

'Warning: isReady() returned false. The plugin will report as "unavailable" until isReady() is implemented.'

The problem: isReady() IS implemented — the scaffolder generates it with `return false`. Saying 'until isReady() is implemented' implies the method doesn't exist, which confuses developers.

Suggested replacement:

'Warning: isReady() returned false — the plugin will report as "unavailable" in the browser. Update isReady() with a real authentication check (e.g., check for session cookies, a DOM element, or a page global).'

This is a one-line string change. No logic changes needed.
